headline: Oil spike may trim global GDP by 0.3%, push inflation higher: Goldman
summary: Investing.com — A surge in oil prices tied to the Iran conflict could shave about 0.3% off global economic growth while pushing inflation higher over the next year, according to analysts at Goldman Sachs.

raw.headline: Oil spike may trim global GDP by 0.3%, push inflation higher: Goldman
raw.summary: Investing.com — A surge in oil prices tied to the Iran conflict could shave about 0.3% off global economic growth while pushing inflation higher over the next year, according to analysts at Goldman Sachs.

headline: Asia-Pacific allies sign $57 billion in deals with U.S. firms, Burgum says
summary: Investing.com — Asia-Pacific allies agreed to $57 billion worth of deals with U.S. companies during the Indo-Pacific Energy Security Forum held in Tokyo this weekend, U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said on Sunday.

raw.headline: Asia-Pacific allies sign $57 billion in deals with U.S. firms, Burgum says
raw.summary: Investing.com — Asia-Pacific allies agreed to $57 billion worth of deals with U.S. companies during the Indo-Pacific Energy Security Forum held in Tokyo this weekend, U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said on Sunday.

headline: IEA says 411.9 million barrels of oil from emergency reserves to be released
summary: Investing.com — Oil from the International Energy Agency’s emergency reserves will begin flowing to global markets soon, after member countries pledged to make available 411.9 million barrels, the agency said in a statement on Sunday.

raw.headline: IEA says 411.9 million barrels of oil from emergency reserves to be released
raw.summary: Investing.com — Oil from the International Energy Agency’s emergency reserves will begin flowing to global markets soon, after member countries pledged to make available 411.9 million barrels, the agency said in a statement on Sunday.

headline: How will the oil disruption matter for consumer spending?
summary: Investing.com – Rising oil prices triggered by supply disruptions and geopolitical tensions could begin to weigh on U.S. consumer spending if the shock persists, according to analysts at Morgan Stanley.

raw.headline: How will the oil disruption matter for consumer spending?
raw.summary: Investing.com – Rising oil prices triggered by supply disruptions and geopolitical tensions could begin to weigh on U.S. consumer spending if the shock persists, according to analysts at Morgan Stanley.

headline: What Would It Take to Tip the Economy into Recession?
summary: Investing.com – A sustained surge in oil prices could push the U.S. economy into recession if it significantly weakens consumer spending and tightens financial conditions, according to a report from Wells Fargo.

raw.headline: What Would It Take to Tip the Economy into Recession?
raw.summary: Investing.com – A sustained surge in oil prices could push the U.S. economy into recession if it significantly weakens consumer spending and tightens financial conditions, according to a report from Wells Fargo.

headline: SLB warns of $0.09 EPS hit as Hormuz blockade shuts down Middle East flows
summary: Investing.com -- Global energy services giant Slb NV (NYSE:SLB) is bracing for a hit to its first-quarter earnings as escalating hostilities in the Middle East disrupt regional operations and effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz.

raw.headline: SLB warns of $0.09 EPS hit as Hormuz blockade shuts down Middle East flows
raw.summary: Investing.com -- Global energy services giant Slb NV (NYSE:SLB) is bracing for a hit to its first-quarter earnings as escalating hostilities in the Middle East disrupt regional operations and effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz.

headline: UBS: AI investment is ‘lone buffer’ for emerging markets as energy costs soar
summary: Investing.com -- Emerging market equities are grappling with a sharp reversal of recent gains as the conflict in the Middle East enters its third week, threatening the growth that has characterized the asset class over the last year. According to a new strategy report from UBS, emerging markets (EM) have become one of the worst-performing asset classes globally since the start of hostilities, as the market shifts into a decisive "risk-off" mode.
